name: High-Tech Crackers
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Hard whole wheat flour; or -more as necessary
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour and salt in a food processor and process for 10 seconds to mix thoroughly.
2. With the motor running, add the warm water in a steady stream, then process for an additional 10 seconds.
3. The dough should form into one large ball; if not, feel the dough. If it feels very sticky, add up to 4 tablespoons more flour and process briefly until a ball forms. If the dough feels dry and floury, restart the processor, add 2 to 3 tablespoons more water, and process until a ball of dough forms.
4. Process for an additional minute without over-processing.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 30 seconds.
6. Cover with plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
7. Preheat the oven to 500°F (260°C) and place two racks near the center of the oven.
8. Divide the dough into 8 pieces. Work with one piece at a time, leaving the others covered.
9. On a lightly floured surface, with lightly floured hands, flatten each piece of dough with your palms.
10. Roll out to a very thin rectangle or round as evenly as possible to ensure even cooking.
11. Gently lift the dough from the rolling surface and place it on a large baking sheet or pizza pan.
12. Sprinkle one of the optional toppings (or leave plain) over the dough.
13. Using a knife or pizza cutter, cut through the dough to make rectangular crackers. Do not worry if they are not all exactly the same size; variations will add interest.
14. Lightly spray the crackers with water and place them on the upper oven rack.
15. Check on the crackers 2 to 3 minutes after placing them in the oven (they may brown from underneath).
16. As soon as the thinnest patches of dough start to brown, remove them from the oven. Continue baking, checking every 30 seconds if necessary, but it is better to remove crackers a little early than too late.
17. Variations in timing are affected by the heat of your oven and how thin you rolled out the dough.
18. Transfer the crackers to a large bowl when cool enough to handle; break up any that need more time to crisp.
19. Roll out the remaining dough, season with toppings, and bake as needed.
20. Store crackers in a well-sealed plastic bag or cookie tins for up to one month. Yield: Approximately 13 dozen very thin crackers of varying size, averaging 1 1/2 x 2 1/2 inches.
